select_when: Blind spots are materially affecting coordination, trust, or self-assessment quality.
Better execution depends on turning feedback and disclosure into specific behavior change instead of vague openness.
A team needs structured self-awareness about what others can see that it cannot yet see for itself.
The work would improve if outside-view input were used to reduce inside-view overconfidence and repeated misdefinition.
avoid_when: Disclosure is becoming performance theater or socially safe sharing rather than the specific feedback and self-revelation needed to reduce the real blind spot.
The model is being used as a standalone substitute for evidence, calibration, or problem-definition discipline.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on blind-spot reduction, feedback uptake, and structured self-awareness rather than widening it into generic openness rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor self-awareness sprawl beyond the raw source's stronger feedback, disclosure, and anti-insularity material.
Did not add higher-order composition fields because this batch remains Wave 1 to Wave 3 only.
open_questions: The output type compresses a source that spans interpersonal communication, leadership calibration, and group decision quality.

select_when.note: The source is explicit that the model is strongest when blind spots are hurting coordination and when structured feedback plus disclosure can improve execution quality.

avoid_when.note: The anti-pattern section directly warns against performative disclosure, vague input, and treating the model as a standalone guide.

input_type.note: The source clearly activates on blind-spot and feedback problems, while the compact type label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The raw source yields better calibration and structured self-awareness, but the typed output label is a compact reviewed abstraction.

reasoning_types.note: The model is naturally reflective about self-knowledge and diagnostic of feedback gaps, while the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
